title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders. Indigenous Achuar, Shuar, Kichwa, Shiwiar, Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa, Akan, Temne homelands do NOT align with modern nation-state boundaries — they pre-date them by centuries or millennia.

Ecuador (modern borders) — guayusa heartland
description: Modern political border. Napo + Pastaza + Morona-Santiago provinces hold the Achuar, Shuar, and Kichwa homelands where guayusa has been cultivated for 1,500+ years. Destruction of primary forest is the direct threat — guayusa cannot be farmed monoculture-style; it needs Inga shade trees and mycorrhizal networks.

Peru (modern borders) — northern Amazonian guayusa range
description: Modern political border. Peru's northern Amazon (Loreto + San Martín) extends the guayusa's natural range. Shared Kichwa and Achuar populations cross the Ecuador-Peru border.

Nigeria (modern borders) — largest kola producer + market
description: Modern political border (post-1960 independence). Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa homelands. Nigeria is the world's largest kola producer and consumer. Lagos is the biggest kola market on Earth.

West African Kola Belt (modern borders) — Sierra Leone to Ghana
description: Modern political borders. Kola's rainforest belt — Sierra Leone, Liberia, Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana, Togo, Benin. Kola trade fueled medieval Mali and Songhai empires via the trans-Saharan caravan network.

Colombia (modern borders) — southern Amazonian guayusa range
description: Modern political border. Colombia's Putumayo Department shares the Amazonian cloud-forest ecology with northern Ecuador — same Kichwa homeland across two nation-states.
